system: simple pendulum at the stable equilibrium under small-angle reduction

practical: encoder, video tracking, or IMU angle estimate near the stable equilibrium

regime_assumptions: theta is small enough that cos(theta) is approximated to second order
air resistance and drive are omitted
equation_hook: U(theta) = m g ell (1 - cos theta) approx 1/2 m g ell theta^2 at theta0 = 0
coordinate_note: The angular coordinate is treated as the declared local coordinate at theta0 = 0; radians are handled as dimensionless for the Hessian.

module_object.description: second derivative of pendulum potential at theta0 = 0

derivation_status: exact for the declared local quadratic approximation at the operating point

ceiling_or_reference.description: no hidden coordinate is declared in this card

positive_scope: This card exposes the local stiffness of the pendulum at a declared stable operating point.
boundary: It does not describe global large-angle pendulum motion.
failure_mode: The attachment fails when the amplitude is large enough that higher-order terms in cos(theta) materially affect the motion.
next_measurement: Measure period at small amplitude and compare the inferred local stiffness with m g ell.
